title: WF-010 Support Ticket to Resolution — school-facing tickets with internal-note privacy
business_reason: Second chain under the workflow-at-a-time rule (WF-010). Support was scaffolded staff-side (tickets, messages with a visibility field, escalations, links) but there was NO school-facing route — a school could not raise or view a ticket. And the chain's core control — internal staff notes must NEVER be visible to the school — had nowhere to be enforced (no school read path). Add the school side and enforce message visibility server-side.

design.routes: POST /api/school/support (raise a ticket — school-scoped, server-generated ticket_code, ticket_source='school', default active category resolved server-side; subject/description required). GET /api/school/support (the school's own tickets). GET /api/school/support/[id] (one own ticket — 404 cross-school — + ONLY school_visible+active messages; internal notes [staff_only/restricted_security] are excluded server-side; the ticket view is whitelisted, school_id not echoed).
design.ui: /school/support page (custom, nav-linked via SCHOOL_PLACEHOLDERS + SCHOOL_CUSTOM_PAGES skip-set): raise-ticket form (UPSTREAM) + tickets table with status + resolution (DOWNSTREAM).
design.control: support_ticket_messages.visibility — the school read path filters to school_visible only; the internal note is provably never returned.

param_14.question: How does a school raise + follow a support ticket without seeing internal staff notes?
param_14.answer: From the school portal Support page a school raises a ticket (server-generated code, default category, ticket_source=school). It then sees its own tickets' status + resolution, and on a ticket only the SCHOOL-VISIBLE messages — internal notes (visibility staff_only/restricted_security) are filtered out server-side and never returned, cross-school access is 404, and internal routing fields are not echoed.
param_14.security_implications: internal-note leakage is the headline risk — enforced server-side (school read path returns only school_visible+active messages); own-school only (cross-school 404, no leak); ticket view whitelisted (no assigned_to/queue/sla/school_id echoed); ticket_source + code are server-set (never client).
param_14.approval_implications: staff resolution/escalation stay staff actions; the school can only raise + read its own tickets.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: school reply (POST a school_visible message); SLA timer worker; notification on resolve; attachments (sensitive_flag) with secure download.
param_14.assumptions: at least one active support_ticket_category exists (seeded).
